items.q: Which type of muscle is under voluntary control?
items.why: Students think all muscles can be controlled if we try hard enough. Cardiac and smooth muscle are involuntary - you can't consciously beat your heart. Only skeletal muscle responds to conscious commands.

items.q: What does the 'I' in FITT stand for?
items.why: Students guess. FITT = Frequency, Intensity, Time, Type - the variables you adjust to apply progressive overload. Intensity = how hard (e.g., % of 1RM or heart rate).

items.q: What causes muscle soreness 1-2 days after intense exercise?
items.correct: Microscopic muscle damage (DOMS), not lactic acid.
items.misconception: Lactic acid still in the muscles.
items.why: Students believe the myth that lactic acid causes soreness. Lactate clears within an hour. Delayed onset muscle soreness (DOMS) is caused by microtrauma from eccentric contractions, peaking 24-72 hours later.
Correct answer: 15-20 minutes. “1 hour” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: Well hydrated. “Too much salt” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Roughly how many ml to drink just before sport?
items.why: The real figure is 200. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
